pages.full_text:
April 10th 2015 Friday.
        Simon, Eve + Tinder left at 6.30pm. at
Thomas + Naomi at Lyttleton Rd, William +Bryony
just moved to Bromyard. Peter + Nana about
to move Calcutta → us → London. John in
Swansea about to move to Cardiff. Charles +
Jenny have just bought a house in Oxford.
I have been spending alot of time in Suffolk
with Annette who is dying.
We drove to __Tonge__ [?H#*] near Nottingham, on
the Cloud Cycleway. Thou the gate wewere
on the cycleway. Warm day.
Saturday   cold morning.  We cycled 3 miles up
to the Trent River, disused, often embanked
railway line thru fields.
        We drove to Nottingham + had lunch
in a pub next to the Kanal. Good, Selaft!
Stag party? next to us. Not many greghans
in restaurant.
        We walked up thru Gatehouse to
the Castle museum. It started as a Norma[n]
pages.notes: The word after 'Tonge' appears to be a correction mark or annotation (possibly '+++' or similar). 'Kanal' appears to be an idiosyncratic spelling of 'Canal'. 'Selaft' is unclear — could be an exclamation or abbreviated word. 'greghans' is unclear — possibly 'grey hairs' or another word. Page text continues onto the right page. No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner. 'Lyttleton Rd' could also read 'Lyttleton Rd' — appears to be a street name. 'at' and partial text visible at right edge belongs to right page.

pages.full_text:
Castle. Charles I raised his banner there 67
at Start of Civil War. Cromwell had it razed.
Rebuilt, burnt, rebuilt (Reform Act).
Lovely alabaster + costume museum.
Tea back at the van + we have driven to
another field – Pleasleyfarm campsite * *.
We went along a Roman road out of
Nottingham – slow + bordered by buildings.
Nottingham used to be called Snottingham.
The Snots.
The farm is a sort of depot for storing
large lorries. Windswept.
                Sunday
                Cold feet all
                night.
                After breakfast
                on to
                Hardwick
                Hall.
pages.notes: Page has two glued-in photos: one large showing an Elizabethan/Jacobean mansion (likely Hardwick Hall given the text context), and one smaller photo below it showing two people. 'Pleasleyfarm campsite' — could also be 'Pleasley Farm campsite' (two words). The handwritten page number '67' is visible in the top-right corner. 'Reform Act' reference explains the rebuilding of Nottingham Castle. 'Sunday' appears underlined.
